title: Diploma in Electric Engineering (Renewable Energy)
content: The program is designed to provide a comprehensive exposure to the skill set required for the renewable energy (Green Energy) revolution. This skill set includes knowledge of renewable energy technologies and practical exposures towards the new technologies emerging in this sector. The module deals with the knowledge of solar power, wind power, tidal, geothermal etc. 10th with minimum 40% marks. For Lateral Entry: 10th (minimum 40%) + 2 year ITI (minimum 40%) shall be eligible for admission to Second Year Diploma Course(s) in Any branch of Engineering and Technology. The programme is designed to provide a comprehensive exposure to the skill set required for the renewable energy (Green Energy) revolution. This skill set includes knowledge of renewable energy technologies and practical exposures towards the new technologies emerging in this sector. The module deals with the knowledge of solar power, wind power ,tidal, geothermal etc . Program Structure01 A Diploma in Electrical Engineering (Renewable Energy) is a three-year diploma course that offers students the knowledge and skills required to design, implement, and optimize electrical systems with a focus on renewable energy technologies. This program emphasizes sustainable practices, hands-on experience with modern tools, and proficiency in various renewable energy sources such as solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and biomass. Graduates will be prepared to address global energy challenges and contribute to the development of sustainable energy solutions. 01 Basic knowledge: An ability to apply knowledge of basic mathematics, science, and engineering to solve engineering problems. 02 Discipline knowledge: An ability to apply discipline-specific knowledge to solve core and/or applied engineering problems. 03 04 Engineering tools: Apply appropriate technologies and tools with an understanding of the limitations. 05 06 Environment and sustainability: Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development. 07 08 Individual and team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings. 09 Communication: An ability to communicate effectively. 10 01 Ability to design, implement, and maintain solar, wind, and biomass energy systems, optimizing their efficiency and integration into electrical grids. 02 Skills in managing and optimizing energy systems for sustainability, including energy storage, smart grids, and demand-side management. 03 Capability to apply knowledge through hands-on training, projects, and internships, driving innovation and adhering to industry standards in renewable energy. 10th with minimum 40% marks. For Lateral Entry: 10th (minimum 40%) + 2 year ITI (minimum 40%) shall be eligible for admission to Second Year Diploma Course(s) in Any branch of Engineering and Technology.
